what_it_does: An acceptance test script that verifies a second known platform (gh-cli) can register, be discovered, classified, and projected through the existing cli-help adapter without editing the core engine, while a valid-but-unbuilt discovery-source type fails loudly and the core engine files contain no platform-specific literals.
observations: Starts with a long narrative docstring framing the file as 'C-GENPROOF' / 'THE LIFT GATE' acceptance.
Defines a simple `ok(name, cond, detail='')` helper that appends results to `PASS` or `FAIL` and prints pass/fail lines.
Case (a) checks that `gh-cli` is discovered by the real `PlatformRegistry` file discovery and that `claude-code` still registers alongside it.
Case (a) spawns the real `gh` binary via `shutil.which` and runs a live DISCOVER→CLASSIFY→PROJECT circuit through `CapabilityRegistry().discover(gh)`.
Case (a) asserts non-zero capability counts, real R5 'safe' postures, and R3 'consent' postures on specific axes (`--repo`→`repo-target`, `--web`→`browser`).
Case (a) checks that `DISCOVERERS` is exactly the closed set `{'cli-help', 'stream-init'}` and that the rows came through `CliHelpDiscoverer`.

Case (b) constructs a `PlatformEntry` with discovery source type `rest-openapi` and invocation kind `rest`, validates it, then expects `run_discover` to raise `MissingAdapterError` naming `RestOpenApiDiscoverer` and the built adapter set.
Case (c) greps `introspection/engine.py`, `rules.py`, `discover.py`, and all `introspection/adapters/*.py` for banned platform-name literals and asserts zero hits.
Uses `PASS`/`FAIL` lists and exits with code 1 if any failure exists.
standouts: Live subprocess proof against the real `gh` binary to demonstrate end-to-end generalization.
Explicit 'zero engine edits' claim tested by both functional behavior and a source-code literal leak grep.
Unbuilt-type test treats a missing adapter as a designed gap-surface rather than a bug.
conventions: Self-contained runnable acceptance script with a narrative docstring tying tests to spec sections.
Uses a minimal `ok()` assertion helper instead of a formal test framework.
concerns: The live `gh pr create --help` output and specific flags (`--repo`, `--web`) may be brittle across `gh` versions or host environments.
The `DISCOVERERS` closed-set assertion will fail if a legitimate new adapter is added, even when the rest of the proof still holds.
Leak grep uses simple substring matching, which could miss obfuscated literals or flag benign occurrences, though the chosen tokens are platform-specific.
If `gh` is not on `PATH`, the headline WORKS case is skipped rather than failing, weakening automated CI guarantees.
notes: A narrative acceptance test that tries to prove the introspection engine is platform-agnostic by adding a second real platform and ensuring missing adapters fail loudly.
questions: What is the exact schema and validation behavior of `PlatformEntry` and its `discovery_sources`/`signal_sets` fields?
How does the posture classifier decide 'safe' vs 'consent' for the specific `--repo` and `--web` flags?
What does the `stream-init` discoverer do, and why is it part of the expected closed adapter set?
How is `platform_registry()` file discovery implemented so that `gh-cli` and `claude-code` are found automatically?
Is the `gh pr create --help` output stable enough for this test across versions and operating systems?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: it is an acceptance suite verifying second-platform registration, engine agnosticism, and loud failure for unbuilt adapters.
apparent_themes: Platform-agnostic core engine with data-driven platform registration.
Fail-loud gap surfaces for unbuilt adapters.
Regression prevention via source-code literal bans.
Generalization proof for a two-level separation.
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to demonstrate that the system's abstraction boundary is real: adding a new platform of a known type should be almost free, while a novel type must produce a clear, actionable error naming the missing adapter, and the core engine must never acquire platform-specific knowledge.
novelty: Combines a live CLI end-to-end proof, schema validation of an unbuilt discovery-source type, and a source-code leak grep into a single executable acceptance narrative.
contribution: Provides an executable, file-local generalization proof and a guard against platform-name leakage in the core engine and adapter layer.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that verifies a second platform (gh-cli) registers and flows through the existing cli-help adapter, that unbuilt adapter types raise MissingAdapterError, and that core engine files contain no platform-specific literals.
intention_for_embedding: To prove the introspection engine is platform-agnostic and that adding platforms is data-driven while missing adapters fail loudly.

symbols.does: Records a pass/fail result, prints a formatted line, and appends the test name to `PASS` or `FAIL`.
symbols.description: A minimal assertion helper used throughout the acceptance script. It takes a test name, a boolean condition, and an optional detail string; when the condition is false it prints the detail and stores the name in the global `FAIL` list, otherwise in `PASS`.
